claim: Create a claude.md system prompt file in your project root to configure Claude Code's behavior and best practices without modifying the model's instructions for each interaction
actionable: Create a .claude/claude.md file with your project patterns and conventions, then reference it in prompts or use /init to generate one automatically
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use nested claude.md files at different directory levels (e.g., frontend/ and backend/) so Claude Code intelligently applies context-specific rules when operating in those folders
actionable: Create claude.md files in subdirectories for different components, and Claude will automatically detect and apply folder-specific instructions
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Set up permission allowlists in settings.local.json for specific bash commands rather than using bash:* which allows all commands without approval
actionable: In .claude/settings.local.json, explicitly list safe commands like git, ls, python but never add rm (remove) without requiring approval
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use slash commands to encapsulate reusable agentic workflows as markdown files in .claude/commands/, allowing parameterized execution with arguments
actionable: Create /commands/primer.md with step-by-step instructions for Claude, then invoke via /primer argument to execute that workflow
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Add the Serena MCP server to enhance Claude Code's semantic code search and understanding, especially for large existing codebases
actionable: Install Serena via 'uvx claude-mcp add serena' and add 'mcp:serena' to permissions in settings.local.json to avoid repeated approval prompts
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use the PRP (Product Requirement Prompt) framework in three steps: define initial requirements in initial.md, generate a comprehensive PRP via /generate command, then execute with /execute-prp
actionable: Create prps/ folder with initial.md describing your feature, run /generate-prp initial.md to create context, then /execute-prp path/to/prp.md to implement
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Create sub-agents with specialized system prompts in .claude/agents/ to handle delegated tasks autonomously, each with its own context window and tool restrictions
actionable: Create agents/validation_gates.md with name, description of when to invoke, and custom tools/model specifications; primary agent calls it based on keywords in prompts
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use Claude Code hooks defined in settings.local.json to trigger deterministic actions before/after tool use, after sub-agent calls, and at other development lifecycle points
actionable: Define hooks in JSON (after_tool_use, before_tool_use) in settings.local.json to run custom bash scripts that log activity or trigger workflows
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use Claude Code with the GitHub CLI (gh) to automatically read issues, implement fixes, run tests, create branches, and open pull requests from a single slash command
actionable: Install GitHub CLI, authenticate with 'gh auth login', create /commands/fix-github-issue.md that reads issue with 'gh issue view', implements fix, validates, and creates PR
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Enable YOLO mode in a devcontainer to run Claude Code entirely autonomously without permission prompts while maintaining safety through container isolation and firewalls
actionable: Add .devcontainer from Anthropic docs to your project, press F1 in VS Code, select 'Reopen in Container', then run 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' inside the isolated environment
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use git worktrees with Claude Code to run multiple parallel agents on different branches simultaneously, allowing you to pick the best implementation
actionable: Use /prep-parallel feature_name 3 to create 3 worktrees with isolated branches, then /execute-parallel feature_name plan.md 3 to run agents in parallel on same feature
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Use keywords like 'important', 'proactively', and 'ultraink' in prompts to Claude Code because they are built into the model and trigger deeper reasoning
actionable: Add these keywords strategically to prompts when you need Claude to spend more tokens thinking through a problem or prioritize certain requirements
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Avoid prompting Claude against keywords like 'production ready' that cause it to overengineer or preserve old code for backward compatibility
actionable: Use terms like 'simplify', 'minimal viable', 'clean slate' when you want Claude to build lean code; be explicit if you need backward compatibility
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies

claim: Store business documents, patterns, and best practices outside claude.md and reference them in global rules to make sharing between team members and switching AI tools easier
actionable: Create separate docs/ folder for patterns, create reference paths in claude.md instead of embedding everything inline, making it portable across teams and tools
source_title: A Complete Guide to Claude Code - Here are ALL the Best Strategies
